summary: Created: CANCEL during model download leaves partial files on disk (no cleanup). Cancel should purge in-flight files (shared-dep protected); PAUSE retains for resume. Repro on remote Pod, SDXL Realistic.

Reframed: likely a STALE DISPLAY bug, not missing cleanup. After uninstalling other models (forced re-sync) the cancelled card corrected 6.6GB->70MB. So cancel probably DOES delete; the card just doesnt re-derive partial bytes until another sync. Verify both: (1) does cancel purge files, (2) does card refresh after cancel (download:cancelled handler calls awaitReSync but partial readout stale).

todo->doing. Root cause found: NOT stale frontend (reSync wiring correct). Remote cancel is soft+async — wrapper sets cancel flag + deletes .part on next chunk, but app re-syncs /wrapper/models/status BEFORE .part removed -> stale partialBytes. Fix: cancel route also calls remoteUninstallDep (synchronous /wrapper/models/delete purges file+.part) for remote deps in the refCount<=0 block.

maturity in-progress->validating. Fix shipped (cancel route calls remoteUninstallDep for remote deps in refCount<=0 block to synchronously purge the .part). Unit-verified: 17/17 remote-hardening+download-completion tests, 0 lint errors. AWAITING live-Pod cancel smoke (purge of remote partial + clean card readout) before -> done/complete.

validating->done (complete/accepted). LIVE-VERIFIED on remote A40 Pod (ephemeral, no volume, models at /cubric-data/mpi_models). Repro: Wan 2.2 Smooth i2v installed (2x14G Wan_22_i2v_High/Low.safetensors), then toggled Text-to-Video on + Update -> t2v High/Low streamed as 13G .part files. Pressed CANCEL. RESULT: both Wan_22_t2v_*.safetensors.part (13G each) PURGED synchronously on cancel (the MPI-123 fix: cancel route -> remoteUninstallDep -> /wrapper/models/delete). i2v files UNTOUCHED (refCount guard held, 14G x2 survived). App card snapped to clean 'INSTALLED 36.5GB/66.5GB' (i2v footprint), NOT stuck at the cancelled t2v bytes -> the original stale-partial-bytes bug is GONE. Toggling t2v off returned card to correct state. BENIGN SUB-FINDING (NOT carded, accepted as inert garbage): orphan <part>.aria2 sidecars (2.1K each) leak on cancel/error/delete -- wrapper.py removes <part> but not <part>.aria2 at 4 sites (1093/1128/1136/1355). CONFIRMED ZERO UI impact: _is_complete_on_disk (wrapper.py:805-824) only checks dest + dest+'.part' for partialBytes, never .aria2, so the orphans are invisible to progress bar / installed-GB / card state. On ephemeral Pod they wipe on Terminate. User decision: leave as garbage, do not address. NOTE: remote Pause is a no-op by design (no resumable .part on Pod) -- use Cancel.
Archived from done column to .agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/_archive/MPI-123/ (board cleanup).
